name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI agents to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3,671M grew 22% YoY, with cRPO of $12.64B growing 22.5% YoY. Serves 8,100+ customers including 85% of the Fortune 500. Market cap approximately $112B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to an enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to the defining enterprise AI platform (2020-present), expanding TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ — a multi-phase business model transformation that few enterprise software companies have achieved [1][2]. The Moveworks acquisition (closed December 2025) and pending Veza acquisition add agentic AI and identity security capabilities. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI customers spending >$1M ACV grew 130%+ YoY in Q1 2026, with AI agents shipping across IT, HR, customer service, and security workflows [1]. The company acquired Moveworks for agentic AI and announced Veza for identity security as autonomous AI-driven workflows scale. Non-GAAP operating margin expanded ~100bps YoY to 32%, partially AI-driven [1]. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the undisputed category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent rival, commanding 85% Fortune 500 penetration and cRPO of $12.64B — the deepest enterprise workflow moat in software [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Armis acquisition expected to close H2 2026 adds cybersecurity TAM; Veza acquisition adds identity security for AI workflows; Arc mainnet launch planned — named products with timelines but revenue contribution still emerging [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow invests heavily in R&D relative to application software peers, with significant AI-focused engineering investment including the Moveworks team integration.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's mission — 'to make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and validated by consistent product execution that removes enterprise friction across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance [2]. The purpose has guided platform expansion decisions and is clearly referenced in CEO McDermott's strategic communications. It differentiates from generic software missions and connects the company's work to human productivity gains. However, it has not been tested through existential crisis or multi-decade leadership transitions. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits and social enterprises, and the company has committed to carbon neutrality and science-based emissions targets — genuine but standard enterprise software CSR [4]. Employee Retention (1/2): ServiceNow consistently ranks in the top tier for enterprise software on employee satisfaction platforms, with CEO approval ratings above 90% and a 29,187-employee workforce that has grown steadily without major layoffs or cultural disruption [2]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO Bill McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares personally in March 2026 during the software sector sell-off and extended his contract to 2030, demonstrating maximum long-term conviction with his own capital [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 delivered subscription revenue of $3,671M (up 22% YoY) with total revenue of $3,770M, and raised FY2026 subscription revenue guidance to $15.735-$15.775B representing 20.5-21% growth [1]. D/E of 20.7% is conservative (score 1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target (score 1.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0). Three-year revenue CAGR of 22.4% exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 21.6 is near the 20 target (score 0.96). Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target but above the 0% floor (score 0.63). PEG of 1.05 is near the 1.0 target (score 0.98, 2x weight). Weighted sum: 9.54/10 = infrastructure score of 10. Exceptional financial profile combining 22%+ growth with operating margin expansion and near-zero leverage [1].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less — CEO McDermott argues that 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions,' and ServiceNow controls the execution layer [2]. The company orchestrates 80B+ enterprise workflows annually, and AI agents need workflow infrastructure to function in production environments. JPMorgan has flagged ServiceNow as an 'AI-resilient' software name, citing its role as connective tissue integrating AI insights into reliable, auditable enterprise actions. However, the risk that agentic AI systems mature to replace rather than require workflow orchestration platforms is non-trivial — if autonomous AI agents can directly execute enterprise actions without ServiceNow's middleware, the disruption thesis reverses. The January 2026 software sector sell-off (IGV down 23%) specifically targeted workflow platforms on this concern [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist AI customers spending >$1M ACV grew 130%+ YoY in Q1 2026, the Moveworks acquisition contributes measurable AI revenue, and the Veza acquisition adds AI-driven identity security — concrete, growing AI-specific revenue contribution [1].

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott operates ServiceNow with genuinely infinite-game thinking — his 'Rule of 55+' standard (revenue growth + FCF margin must exceed 55) enforces long-term capital discipline, his contract extension to 2030 and $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 sell-off demonstrate leadership conviction, and the company's consistent platform expansion from ITSM to enterprise AI reflects purpose-driven evolution rather than quarter-by-quarter optimization. ServiceNow's Just Cause of eliminating enterprise friction is validated by sustained 20%+ growth and 85% Fortune 500 penetration.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites technology partners and customers to contribute. McDermott has articulated this cause consistently across economic cycles and competitive environments. However, it has not been tested through existential crisis or multi-decade leadership transitions in the way Sinek's canonical examples have — ServiceNow is still in its growth phase under its second major CEO.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow consistently ranks in the top tier for enterprise software on employee satisfaction metrics, with CEO approval ratings above 90% [2]. The company's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates organizational psychological safety during an era when many software employees fear displacement. The 29,187-employee workforce has grown steadily without major layoffs or cultural disruption, and the company's investment in employee AI training signals trust in workforce capability. McDermott's leadership style emphasizes empowerment and customer obsession.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as rivals whose strengths push ServiceNow to improve — the Moveworks acquisition (AI customer service) and Veza acquisition (identity security) directly reflect competitive awareness of gaps revealed by rivals' capabilities [2]. The company frames competitive dynamics as motivation to expand platform capabilities rather than warfare to destroy competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The transformation from ITSM ticketing to enterprise workflow platform to enterprise AI platform represents significant strategic evolution that expanded TAM 10x+ [2]. Each phase built on the previous while redefining the category. However, these were incremental platform extensions — ServiceNow never truly abandoned its core product; it layered capabilities on top of it. A true existential flex (Sinek's standard) would involve abandoning a profitable core business, which ServiceNow has not done.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout — when NOW dropped 10%+ on AI disruption fears — is precisely Sinek's definition of courage: acting in service of the Just Cause at personal financial cost [2]. The Rule of 55+ standard maintains long-term capital discipline even when it constrains short-term growth. The acquisitions of Moveworks, Armis, and Veza during market uncertainty represent bold capability bets. McDermott's 2030 contract extension during the AI uncertainty period further demonstrates sustained commitment.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI revolution. Q1 2026 delivered subscription revenue of $3,671M (up 22% YoY), cRPO of $12.64B (up 22.5%), and non-GAAP operating margin expanding 100bps to 32%, while management raised FY2026 guidance to $15.735-$15.775B [1]. Now Assist AI customers spending >$1M ACV grew 130%+ YoY, validating the AI upsell thesis. The Moveworks acquisition adds agentic AI capabilities, Veza adds identity security for autonomous workflows, and Armis (H2 2026 close) expands into cybersecurity. At a PEG of 1.05 and forward P/E of 21.6x (post-split), ServiceNow offers rare combination of 20%+ growth with near-best-in-class operating leverage [1][2].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on two risks. First, the long-term threat that agentic AI systems mature to execute enterprise actions autonomously without requiring ServiceNow's workflow orchestration middleware — if AI agents can directly interact with backend systems, ServiceNow's 'execution layer' thesis inverts from enabler to redundant intermediary [2]. The January 2026 sell-off (IGV down 23%) specifically targeted this concern. Second, the trailing P/E of 64x reflects GAAP profit margin of only 12.6% on $14B in revenue — stock-based compensation remains a significant dilutive drag. GAAP profitability must expand materially for the valuation to be sustained if growth decelerates from 22% toward the guided 20.5-21% range. The Armis and Veza acquisition integration risks add execution complexity during a critical AI transition period.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI customer spending >$1M ACV continuing to grow 100%+ YoY in Q2-Q4 2026, validating AI as a durable revenue driver rather than a one-time upgrade cycle
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026 and Veza identity security acquisition adding measurable incremental revenue and expanding the platform TAM to security operations
Non-GAAP operating margin expanding beyond 32% in FY2026 while maintaining 20%+ revenue growth, sustaining the Rule of 55+ standard and improving GAAP profitability trajectory
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration platforms within 2-3 years, faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates — the January 2026 sell-off demonstrated market concern about this scenario
GAAP profit margin of only 12.6% on $14B revenue due to significant stock-based compensation — at 64x trailing P/E, any growth deceleration below 20% would trigger severe multiple compression
Integration complexity from three concurrent acquisitions (Moveworks, Armis, Veza) during a critical AI platform transition, creating execution risk in a premium-valuation stock

ServiceNow Board Governance and AI Oversight — SEC DEF 14A Proxy Statement, April 2026
